title: Lathyrus montanus lusus albiflorus
taxonomy: Lathyrus montanus lusus albiflorus is a variant of Lathyrus montanus, a perennial legume native to the mountains of the Iberian Peninsula. The classification of Lathyrus montanus lusus albiflorus falls under the family Fabaceae and its binomial nomenclature is a subset of the Lathyrus genus. As a variant, it shares the same general classification as Lathyrus montanus but may have distinct characteristics in its appearance or growth habits. Lathyrus montanus lusus albiflorus is a rare and lesser-known variant of the species. The exact classification of this variant may require further research and identification.

intro: Lathyrus montanus lusus albiflorus, also known as the white-flowered Alpine pea, is a variant of the perennial legume Lathyrus montanus, native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with cardiovascular disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Lathyrus montanus lusus albiflorus include isoorientin, a flavonoid glycoside with demonstrated antioxidant activity, as well as other phenolic compounds and alkaloids. Traditionally, this plant has been used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, often in combination with other herbs, to support overall health and well-being.
